title: React Beginner Specialist Mock Test
description: Foundational assessment for entry-level React developers. Covers JSX, component architecture, props, state, and basic hooks.

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of the Virtual DOM?
options: Direct access to the browser's hardware
Faster rendering by minimizing real DOM updates
questions.explanation: React uses the Virtual DOM to calculate the most efficient way to update the browser's UI.

questions.text: How do you pass a piece of data from a parent to a child component?
questions.explanation: Props (properties) are the standard way to pass data down the component tree.

questions.text: Which hook is used to add local state to a functional component?
questions.explanation: useState is the fundamental hook for managing state in functional components.

questions.text: In JSX, which attribute is used instead of the standard 'class' attribute?
questions.explanation: Because 'class' is a reserved keyword in JavaScript, React uses 'className'.

questions.text: What is the correct way to write an expression inside JSX?
questions.explanation: Curly braces are used to evaluate JavaScript expressions within JSX markup.

questions.text: When using the `useEffect` hook with an empty dependency array `[]`, when does it run?
options: Only when the component is unmounted
Only once, after the initial render
questions.explanation: An empty dependency array tells React to run the effect only once after the component mounts.

questions.text: What is the 'key' prop used for when rendering lists?
options: To uniquely identify elements for efficient updates
To set the index of the array
questions.explanation: Keys help React identify which items in a list have changed, been added, or removed.

questions.text: How do you handle a click event in React?
questions.explanation: React events use camelCase and pass a function reference.

questions.text: What does a React component return?
questions.explanation: Components return React elements, usually written in JSX, which describe the UI.

questions.text: Which tool is commonly used to bundle and manage dependencies in a React project?
questions.explanation: npm and yarn are package managers used to install and manage libraries.

questions.text: What is the 'lifting state up' pattern?
options: Moving state to a common parent to share it between siblings
Moving state to the global window object
questions.explanation: Lifting state up is the standard way to share data between sibling components.

questions.text: What is a 'Fragment' in React?
options: A way to group elements without adding extra DOM nodes
A specific type of CSS file
questions.explanation: Fragments (<> or <React.Fragment>) let you group children without adding extra div wrappers.

questions.text: True or False: React components must start with a capital letter.
questions.explanation: React uses capitalization to distinguish custom components from standard HTML tags.

questions.text: How do you conditionally render a component in React?
questions.explanation: Ternaries and logical AND are the standard ways to toggle UI elements in JSX.

questions.text: What is the 'children' prop?
options: A list of child components' names
The content passed between the opening and closing tags of a component
A function to create new components
A way to access a database
questions.explanation: Props.children allows you to nest components inside each other.

questions.text: Which hook is used to access a DOM element directly?
questions.explanation: useRef returns a mutable ref object whose .current property can hold a reference to a DOM node.

questions.text: What does 'Pure Component' mean in React?
options: A component that renders the same output for the same props/state
A component that uses only HTML
questions.explanation: Purity ensures that the UI is predictable and can be optimized for performance.

questions.text: What is the purpose of `npm start` in a Create React App project?
options: To build the project for production
questions.explanation: npm start launches the local environment for real-time coding.

questions.text: Can you use Hooks inside a Class component?
questions.explanation: Hooks are designed for functional components and cannot be used in class components.

questions.text: What is the default port for a Create React App development server?
questions.explanation: By default, React apps run on http://localhost:3000.
